content.problemStatement:
Let's say that we have characters $C_1$, $C_2$, ..., $C_n$ with frequencies $F_1$, $F_2$, ... $F_N$ respectively.

Let the length of the string we are constructing be $Len$.

Now imagine constructing the string by placing the characters serial-wise. \
First we will place character $C_1$, we have $Len$ places. (Say - stage 1)\
Then, we will place character $C_2$, we have $Len$ $-$ $F_1$ places. (Say - stage 1) \
. \
. \
At last, we will place the character $C_N$, we will have $Len$ $-$ $\sum_{i = 0}^{N - 1} F_i$ places. (Say - stage N)

Notice that at every stage (say $k$), we will have $Len$ $-$ $\sum_{i = 0}^{k - 1} F_{i}$ number of places to fill; irrespective of how we have filled the characters at previous stages.

So these actions are Independent - we will apply the rule of product here.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
